title: Amalgamation of India Radiators Limited into Mercantile Ventures Limited -
  Record Date Fixed
---

## Summary

BSE has informed trading members that pursuant to the Scheme of Amalgamation approved by the Hon'ble National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), Chennai Bench, India Radiators Limited (Transferor Company) will be merged into Mercantile Ventures Limited (Transferee Company). The Record Date for determining shareholder entitlement has been fixed as 24 July 2026, and trading in India Radiators Limited shares will be restricted/suspended from the same date.

## Key Points

- Scheme of Amalgamation approved by NCLT, Chennai Bench, under reference DR-679/2026-2027
- India Radiators Limited (Scrip Code: 505100) to be merged into Mercantile Ventures Limited
- Shareholders of India Radiators Ltd will receive 10 equity shares of Mercantile Ventures Limited (face value Rs. 10 each, fully paid up) for every 36 equity shares held in India Radiators Ltd (face value Rs. 10 each, fully paid up)
- Trading in India Radiators Ltd shares is restricted on account of GSM (Graded Surveillance Measure)
- The scrip will not be available for trading from 24 July 2026 due to the amalgamation corporate action

## Regulatory Changes

No new regulatory changes; this circular implements a previously NCLT-approved Scheme of Amalgamation by notifying the record date and consequent trading suspension.

## Compliance Requirements

Trading members must note that dealings in India Radiators Ltd equity shares are suspended from 24 July 2026. Shareholders need not take further action; entitlement to Mercantile Ventures Limited shares will be determined based on holdings as of the record date.

## Important Dates

- Record Date: 24 July 2026
- No Dealings/Suspension effective from: 24 July 2026

## Impact Assessment

This is a high-impact corporate action for shareholders of India Radiators Limited, as their shares will cease trading and be replaced by an entitlement to Mercantile Ventures Limited shares at a 10:36 swap ratio. Trading members must update systems to reflect the suspension and ensure no further dealings occur in the transferor company's scrip after the record date.
